text:
we would expect agent1's mention of agent2 to be immediately injected into agent2's session (since it's running via tenex-edge launch and thus it auto-injects the message as user message, properly attributed to agent1 (i.e. agent2 needs to know this message came from agent1) [...] if it works it would need to be injected in the userPrompt hook [...] if it works ok, agent2 would use tenex-edge chat write to reply back, mentioning agent1

since agent1 is not running on a tenex-edge launch the new message won't trigger a user message injection -- so you, as the user, would need to ask afterwards "did you receive something? dont use any tools -- if the reply back is not injected in your context let me know"
